title: Today's AI Brief: Apple Approves Poke as the First AI Agent on its Messages for Business Platform
summary: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform and OpenAI outlines its public policy agenda for AI.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
imageCaption: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
imageSourceTitle: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform

body.text: I'm checking out some fresh signals in the world of AI. Apple has just approved Poke, a startup that makes AI agents super easy to use, to run on its Messages for Business platform. This is a pretty big deal, as it brings AI-powered interactions to a widely used messaging platform. I mean, who doesn't love a good chatbot?
Poke on Apple's Messages for Business

Poke's AI agent is designed to make interactions as simple as sending a text message. What's fascinating here is that Apple is opening up its platform to AI agents, which could change how businesses interact with customers. For instance, imagine being able to book a flight or make a reservation with just a few taps. It's like having a super-smart assistant at your fingertips.

Meanwhile, OpenAI has outlined its public policy agenda for AI, focusing on safety, youth protection, workforce transition, and global standards. It's a comprehensive approach to ensuring AI benefits society. I'm glad to see them prioritizing these areas, as it shows they're thinking about the broader implications of AI. It's not just about building cool tech; it's about building tech that works for everyone.

Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Poke , a startup that turns using AI agents into something as simple as sending a text message, has become the first AI agent approved to run on Apple’s Messages for Business platform. Previously, the platform was designed for businesses — airlines, retailers, hotel chains, and others — to communicate with their own customers through iMessage, offering a st... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

OpenAI public policy agenda is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: OpenAI outlines its public policy agenda for AI, including safety, youth protection, workforce transition, and global standards to ensure AI benefits society. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
